creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Dan Naumov from comment #0)
> Right now, manually creating properly aligned partitions for optimal use with
> NAND-based media is a truly painful process.

It should (now) be as easy as adding `-a 1M` to the `gpart add` command, e.g. `gpart add -a 1M -t freebsd-ufs md0`.

It looks like there could be some improvements to be made in the installer.  ZFS with ZFSBOOT_FORCE_4K_SECTORS set will use `-a 4k` for the boot partitions and `-a 1m` for the main partition(s).  As far as I can tell all other partitioning does not set an alignment.
